1.title: Fuzzy Zoeller wins Greater Greensboro Open
1.text: Fuzzy Zoeller closed strong to win the Greater Greensboro Open, a springboard to his Masters playoff victory just weeks later in 1979.

1.title: Greg Norman wins Doral-Ryder Open era aside
1.text: Greg Norman dominated the early 1990 PGA Tour season as World No. 1, a stretch that included multiple titles in Florida's spring swing.

1.title: David Toms wins Compaq Classic of New Orleans
1.text: David Toms shot 22-under to win the Compaq Classic of New Orleans by one stroke, part of a four-win 2001 season that included his PGA Championship.

1.title: Adam Scott wins Shell Houston Open
1.text: Adam Scott won the 2007 Shell Houston Open, a tune-up the week before the Masters during his rise up the world rankings.

1.title: Hunter Mahan wins Houston Open
1.text: Hunter Mahan beat Carl Pettersson by two strokes to win the 2012 Shell Houston Open the week before the Masters.

1.title: Ian Poulter wins Houston Open in playoff
1.text: Ian Poulter beat Beau Hossler in a playoff to win the 2018 Houston Open, earning the final Masters spot the week before Augusta.

2.title: Jack Nicklaus wins Greater Greensboro Open
2.text: Jack Nicklaus won the Greater Greensboro Open in 1972, one of seven PGA Tour titles he claimed that dominant season.

2.title: Steve Jones wins MCI Heritage Classic
2.text: Steve Jones won the MCI Heritage Classic at Harbour Town in 1989, an early career title for the future 1996 U.S. Open champion.

2.title: Peter Jacobsen wins back-to-back early-season titles
2.text: Peter Jacobsen was in the form of his life in early 1995, capturing consecutive PGA Tour titles in the AT&T Pebble Beach and Buick Invitational stretch.

2.title: Tiger Woods wins Bay Hill Invitational
2.text: Tiger Woods birdied the 72nd hole to win the 2000 Bay Hill Invitational by one over Davis Love III, part of his historic 2000 season.

2.title: Geoff Ogilvy wins Doral era aside
2.text: Geoff Ogilvy emerged as a force in 2006, a year that would culminate in his U.S. Open victory at Winged Foot that June.

2.title: Russell Henley wins Houston Open
2.text: Russell Henley shot a final-round 65 to win the 2017 Shell Houston Open by three strokes the week before the Masters.

3.title: Ben Crenshaw wins Byron Nelson Classic
3.text: Ben Crenshaw won the Byron Nelson Golf Classic in 1983, one of 19 PGA Tour titles for the two-time Masters champion.

3.title: Sandy Lyle's banner 1988 season
3.text: Sandy Lyle was the world's hottest player in early 1988, a run that would peak with his green jacket at Augusta National that April.

3.title: Nick Price's dominant year begins
3.text: Nick Price was building toward a 1994 season in which he won both the Open Championship and PGA Championship and topped the world rankings.

3.title: Vijay Singh wins Shell Houston Open
3.text: Vijay Singh won the 2005 Shell Houston Open during his prime as World No. 1, beating a strong field at Redstone Golf Club.

3.title: Gary Woodland wins Transitions Championship era aside
3.text: Long-hitting Gary Woodland broke through for his first PGA Tour title in the spring of 2011, beginning a career that included the 2019 U.S. Open.

3.title: Jim Herman wins Houston Open
3.text: Jim Herman won the 2016 Shell Houston Open for his first PGA Tour title to earn a place in that year's Masters.

4.title: Arnold Palmer wins Masters for fourth time
4.text: Arnold Palmer closed with a 70 to win the 1964 Masters by six strokes over Dave Marr and Jack Nicklaus, his fourth and final green jacket.

4.title: Jack Nicklaus wins Masters with record 271
4.text: Jack Nicklaus shot a then-record 271 (-17) to win the 1965 Masters by nine strokes, prompting Bobby Jones's famous remark about a game with which he was not familiar.

4.title: Charles Coody wins the Masters
4.text: Charles Coody birdied three of the last six holes to win the 1971 Masters by two strokes over Johnny Miller and Jack Nicklaus.

4.title: Craig Stadler wins the Masters
4.text: Craig Stadler held on after a back-nine wobble to beat Dan Pohl in a sudden-death playoff and win the 1982 Masters for his only major.

4.title: Greg Norman wins Players Championship
4.text: Greg Norman won the 1993 Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass during a stretch in which he returned to the top of the world rankings.

4.title: Jose Maria Olazabal wins second Masters
4.text: Jose Maria Olazabal completed a comeback from a foot ailment to win the 1999 Masters by two strokes over Davis Love III.

5.title: Art Wall Jr. wins the Masters
5.text: Art Wall Jr. birdied five of the last six holes for a closing 66 to win the 1959 Masters by one stroke over Cary Middlecoff.

5.title: Billy Casper wins the Masters
5.text: Billy Casper tied Gene Littler over 72 holes and won the 1970 Masters the next day in an 18-hole playoff, his only green jacket and third major title.

5.title: Tom Watson wins second Masters
5.text: Tom Watson held off Jack Nicklaus and Johnny Miller to win the 1981 Masters by two strokes for his second green jacket.

5.title: Larry Mize wins Masters with chip-in
5.text: Larry Mize holed a 140-foot pitch on the 11th to beat Greg Norman in a sudden-death playoff and win the 1987 Masters, stunning the field at Augusta National.

5.title: Fred Couples wins the Masters
5.text: Fred Couples, whose ball improbably hung on the bank above Rae's Creek at the 12th, won the 1992 Masters by two strokes over Ray Floyd for his lone major.

5.title: Mark O'Meara wins the Masters
5.text: Mark O'Meara birdied the 17th and 18th to win the 1998 Masters by one stroke over Fred Couples and David Duval, his first major at age 41.

6.title: Jimmy Demaret wins second Masters
6.text: Jimmy Demaret won the 1947 Masters by two strokes over Byron Nelson and Frank Stranahan, the second of his three green jackets.

6.title: Arnold Palmer wins first Masters
6.text: Arnold Palmer won the 1958 Masters by one stroke over Doug Ford and Fred Hawkins for his first major, amid a famous embedded-ball ruling at the 12th.

6.title: George Archer wins the Masters
6.text: George Archer, one of the era's best putters, won the 1969 Masters by one stroke over Billy Casper, George Knudson and Tom Weiskopf.

6.title: Jack Nicklaus wins sixth Masters at 46
6.text: Jack Nicklaus shot a final-round 65 with a back nine of 30 to win the 1986 Masters by one, becoming the oldest champion at 46 for his record 18th major.

6.title: Tiger Woods sets Masters record
6.text: Tiger Woods closed at 18-under 270 to win the 1997 Masters by a record 12 strokes, becoming the youngest champion at 21 and the tournament's first Black winner.

6.title: Mike Weir wins the Masters in playoff
6.text: Canadian Mike Weir beat Len Mattiace on the first playoff hole to win the 2003 Masters, the first left-hander and first Canadian to win at Augusta.

6.title: Trevor Immelman wins the Masters
6.text: South African Trevor Immelman held off Tiger Woods by three strokes to win the 2008 Masters for his only major title.

7.title: Gene Sarazen's double eagle ties Masters
7.text: Gene Sarazen holed a 235-yard 4-wood for an albatross at the par-5 15th in the final round of the 1935 Masters, the 'shot heard round the world,' to force a 36-hole playoff he won the next day.

7.title: Doug Ford wins the Masters
7.text: Doug Ford holed out from a greenside bunker on the 72nd hole for a closing 66 to win the 1957 Masters by three strokes over Sam Snead.

7.title: Jack Nicklaus wins first Masters
7.text: Jack Nicklaus, at 23, won the 1963 Masters by one stroke over Tony Lema, becoming the youngest Masters champion at the time.

7.title: Bob Goalby wins Masters amid scorecard error
7.text: Bob Goalby won the 1968 Masters by one stroke after Roberto De Vicenzo, celebrating his 45th birthday, signed for a 4 instead of a 3 at the 17th and lost a chance at a playoff.

7.title: Gary Player wins second Masters
7.text: Gary Player won the 1974 Masters by two strokes over Tom Weiskopf and Dave Stockton for the second of his three green jackets.

7.title: Bernhard Langer wins first Masters
7.text: Germany's Bernhard Langer won the 1985 Masters by two strokes over Curtis Strange, Seve Ballesteros and Ray Floyd for his first major title.

7.title: Tiger Woods defends Masters title
7.text: Tiger Woods won the 2002 Masters by three strokes over Retief Goosen, becoming the third player to successfully defend a green jacket.

8.title: Sarazen wins Masters playoff
8.text: Gene Sarazen defeated Craig Wood by five strokes in a 36-hole playoff to win the 1935 Masters, a day after his famous double eagle.

8.title: Jack Burke Jr. wins the Masters
8.text: Jack Burke Jr. rallied from eight strokes back on the final day to win the 1956 Masters by one over amateur Ken Venturi, one of the great Augusta comebacks.

8.title: Arnold Palmer wins Masters in playoff
8.text: Arnold Palmer beat Gary Player and Dow Finsterwald in an 18-hole playoff to win the 1962 Masters for his third green jacket.

8.title: Tommy Aaron wins the Masters
8.text: Tommy Aaron shot a closing 68 to win the 1973 Masters by one stroke over J.C. Snead for his only major championship.

8.title: Ben Crenshaw wins first Masters
8.text: Ben Crenshaw won the 1984 Masters by two strokes over Tom Watson for an emotional first major title after years of near-misses.

8.title: Bubba Watson wins Masters in playoff
8.text: Bubba Watson hooked a wedge from the pine straw to within feet of the cup and beat Louis Oosthuizen on the second playoff hole to win the 2012 Masters, his first major.

8.title: Patrick Reed wins the Masters
8.text: Patrick Reed held off Rickie Fowler and Jordan Spieth to win the 2018 Masters by one stroke for his only major title.

9.title: Ralph Guldahl wins the Masters
9.text: Ralph Guldahl, already a two-time U.S. Open champion, won the 1939 Masters by one stroke over Sam Snead.

9.title: Jimmy Demaret wins third Masters
9.text: Jimmy Demaret rallied to win the 1950 Masters by two strokes over Jim Ferrier, becoming the first three-time Masters champion.

9.title: Seve Ballesteros born
9.text: Spain's Seve Ballesteros, a five-time major champion and one of golf's most charismatic figures, was born on this day in Pedrena.

9.title: Gary Player wins first Masters
9.text: Gary Player won the 1961 Masters by one stroke over Arnold Palmer and amateur Charles Coe, becoming the first international winner at Augusta National.

9.title: Gay Brewer wins the Masters
9.text: Gay Brewer, who had lost a playoff the year before, won the 1967 Masters by one stroke over Bobby Nichols for his only major.

9.title: Gary Player wins third Masters at 42
9.text: Gary Player shot a final-round 64 to come from seven back and win the 1978 Masters by one stroke, his third green jacket at age 42.

9.title: Nick Faldo wins first Masters in playoff
9.text: Nick Faldo beat Scott Hoch on the second playoff hole in the gloaming to win the 1989 Masters, the first of his three green jackets.

9.title: Jon Rahm wins the Masters
9.text: Jon Rahm shot a final-round 69 to win the 2023 Masters by four strokes over Phil Mickelson and Brooks Koepka, returning to World No. 1.

10.title: Cary Middlecoff wins the Masters
10.text: Cary Middlecoff, aided by an 80-foot eagle putt at the 13th, won the 1955 Masters by a then-record seven strokes over Ben Hogan.

10.title: Arnold Palmer birdies last two to win Masters
10.text: Arnold Palmer birdied the 71st and 72nd holes to win the 1960 Masters by one stroke over Ken Venturi for his second green jacket.

10.title: Jack Nicklaus first to defend Masters
10.text: Jack Nicklaus beat Tommy Jacobs and Gay Brewer in an 18-hole playoff to win the 1966 Masters, the first player to successfully defend the title.

10.title: Tom Watson wins first Masters
10.text: Tom Watson outdueled Jack Nicklaus down the stretch to win the 1977 Masters by two strokes, a prelude to their epic Open duel at Turnberry that summer.

10.title: Seve Ballesteros wins second Masters
10.text: Seve Ballesteros birdied four of the first four holes Sunday and cruised to win the 1983 Masters by four strokes over Ben Crenshaw and Tom Kite.

10.title: Tiger Woods wins Masters after chip-in at 16
10.text: Tiger Woods holed an iconic chip on the 16th, the ball pausing on the lip before dropping, then beat Chris DiMarco in a playoff to win the 2005 Masters for his fourth green jacket.

10.title: Scottie Scheffler wins first Masters
10.text: Scottie Scheffler, the new World No. 1, won the 2022 Masters by three strokes over Rory McIlroy for his first major championship.

11.title: Sam Snead wins Masters playoff over Hogan
11.text: Sam Snead beat Ben Hogan by one stroke in an 18-hole playoff to win the 1954 Masters, the third and final green jacket of his career.

11.title: Ray Floyd wins Masters with record
11.text: Ray Floyd led wire-to-wire and tied the Masters scoring record at 271 (-17) to win the 1976 Masters by eight strokes over Ben Crenshaw.

11.title: Craig Stadler's Masters playoff
11.text: Craig Stadler held off Dan Pohl in sudden death to win the 1982 Masters this week, the 'Walrus' claiming his only major championship.

11.title: Bernhard Langer wins second Masters
11.text: Bernhard Langer won the 1993 Masters by four strokes over Chip Beck for the second of his two green jackets.

11.title: Phil Mickelson wins first major at Masters
11.text: Phil Mickelson holed an 18-foot birdie putt on the 72nd hole to win the 2004 Masters by one over Ernie Els, ending his long wait for a first major.

11.title: Phil Mickelson wins third Masters
11.text: Phil Mickelson, after a brilliant shot from the pine straw at the 13th, won the 2010 Masters by three strokes in an emotional victory amid his wife's cancer battle.

11.title: Hideki Matsuyama wins the Masters
11.text: Hideki Matsuyama won the 2021 Masters by one stroke over Will Zalatoris, becoming the first Japanese man to win a major championship.

12.title: Ben Hogan wins Masters in record year
12.text: Ben Hogan shot a record 274 (-14) to win the 1953 Masters by five strokes, the first of three majors he won that historic season.

12.title: Tom Watson's second green jacket
12.text: Tom Watson completed his second Masters victory in 1981 this week, holding off a charging Jack Nicklaus down the stretch at Augusta National.

12.title: Larry Mize's miracle chip
12.text: Larry Mize's 140-foot chip-in at the 11th to beat Greg Norman in the 1987 Masters playoff remains one of the most shocking shots in major history.

12.title: Fred Couples and the ball at the 12th
12.text: Fred Couples won the 1992 Masters this week after his tee shot at the par-3 12th miraculously stayed on the bank instead of rolling into Rae's Creek.

12.title: Angel Cabrera wins Masters in playoff
12.text: Argentina's Angel Cabrera beat Kenny Perry and Chad Campbell in a sudden-death playoff to win the 2009 Masters for his second major.

12.title: Jordan Spieth wins Masters with record-tying 270
12.text: Jordan Spieth led wire-to-wire and tied the tournament scoring record at 270 (-18) to win the 2015 Masters by four strokes for his first major.

13.title: Jack Nicklaus wins fifth Masters
13.text: Jack Nicklaus outlasted Tom Weiskopf and Johnny Miller by one stroke in a celebrated duel to win the 1975 Masters for his fifth green jacket.

13.title: Seve Ballesteros wins first Masters
13.text: Seve Ballesteros, at 23, won the 1980 Masters by four strokes to become the youngest Masters champion at the time and the first European winner.

13.title: Nicklaus's Sunday charge at the Masters
13.text: On this Sunday in 1986, Jack Nicklaus's back-nine 30 carried him to his sixth Masters at age 46, one of the most celebrated final rounds in golf history.

13.title: Tiger Woods's record Masters Sunday
13.text: On this day Tiger Woods completed his 12-stroke, 18-under romp at the 1997 Masters, the most dominant performance in tournament history.

13.title: Trevor Immelman holds off Tiger
13.text: Trevor Immelman completed a three-stroke win over Tiger Woods at the 2008 Masters this week for his only major championship.

13.title: Rory McIlroy completes career Grand Slam
13.text: Rory McIlroy beat Justin Rose on the first playoff hole to win the 2025 Masters, completing the career Grand Slam as the sixth man to achieve it.

14.title: Roberto De Vicenzo born
14.text: Argentina's Roberto De Vicenzo, the 1967 Open champion who famously lost the 1968 Masters to a scorecard error on his 45th birthday, was born on this day.

14.title: Ben Hogan wins first Masters
14.text: Ben Hogan won the 1951 Masters by two strokes over Skee Riegel for the first of his two green jackets.

14.title: Goalby wins as De Vicenzo signs wrong card
14.text: On his 45th birthday, Roberto De Vicenzo signed an incorrect scorecard at the 1968 Masters, and Bob Goalby was awarded the green jacket by a single stroke.

14.title: Nick Faldo wins as Norman collapses
14.text: Nick Faldo shot 67 and overtook Greg Norman, who shot 78 after a six-shot lead, to win the 1996 Masters by five strokes in one of golf's greatest final-day swings.

14.title: Tiger Woods defends Masters title
14.text: Tiger Woods won the 2002 Masters by three strokes over Retief Goosen, becoming the third player to successfully defend a Masters title.

14.title: Tiger Woods wins Masters comeback
14.text: Tiger Woods won the 2019 Masters by one stroke for his fifth green jacket and 15th major, completing one of the greatest comebacks in sports after years of injuries.

14.title: Scottie Scheffler wins second Masters
14.text: Scottie Scheffler won the 2024 Masters by four strokes over Ludvig Aberg for his second green jacket while ranked World No. 1.

15.title: Jack Burke's record comeback
15.text: Jack Burke Jr.'s 1956 Masters win from eight strokes back on the final day remains the largest final-round comeback in tournament history.

15.title: Arnold Palmer's playoff charge
15.text: Arnold Palmer's playoff win over Gary Player and Dow Finsterwald at the 1962 Masters was another chapter in the 'Palmer charge' lore at Augusta.

15.title: Nick Faldo wins second straight Masters
15.text: Nick Faldo beat Ray Floyd on the second playoff hole to win the 1990 Masters, becoming the first to win back-to-back since Jack Nicklaus in 1965-66.

15.title: Tiger Woods completes the Tiger Slam
15.text: Tiger Woods won the 2001 Masters by two strokes over David Duval to hold all four major titles at once, an achievement dubbed the 'Tiger Slam.'

15.title: Zach Johnson wins cold, windy Masters
15.text: Zach Johnson won the 2007 Masters at one-over-par, the highest winning total relative to par in the modern era, laying up on every par-5 all week.

15.title: Satoshi Kodaira wins RBC Heritage
15.text: Japan's Satoshi Kodaira beat Si Woo Kim on the third playoff hole to win the 2018 RBC Heritage at Harbour Town for his first PGA Tour title.

16.title: Jack Nicklaus wins fourth Masters
16.text: Jack Nicklaus won the 1972 Masters by three strokes over Bruce Crampton, Bobby Mitchell and Tom Weiskopf for his fourth green jacket.

16.title: Gary Player's closing 64
16.text: Gary Player's final-round 64 to win the 1978 Masters from seven shots back remains one of the greatest closing rounds in major championship history.

16.title: Vijay Singh wins the Masters
16.text: Vijay Singh won the 2000 Masters by three strokes over Ernie Els, adding a green jacket to his 1998 PGA Championship.

16.title: Phil Mickelson wins second Masters
16.text: Phil Mickelson won the 2006 Masters by two strokes over Tim Clark for his second green jacket and third major title.

16.title: Sergio Garcia wins the Masters
16.text: Sergio Garcia beat Justin Rose on the first playoff hole to win the 2017 Masters, his first major, on what would have been Seve Ballesteros's 60th birthday.

16.title: Matt Fitzpatrick wins RBC Heritage
16.text: Matt Fitzpatrick beat Jordan Spieth on the third playoff hole to win the 2023 RBC Heritage at Harbour Town for his second PGA Tour title.

17.title: Sam Snead wins first Masters
17.text: Sam Snead won the 1949 Masters by three strokes, the first edition in which the champion received the now-iconic green jacket.

17.title: Sandy Lyle wins the Masters
17.text: Scotland's Sandy Lyle holed a long bunker shot at the 18th, then made the birdie putt to win the 1988 Masters by one, the first British winner at Augusta.

17.title: Jose Maria Olazabal wins first Masters
17.text: Jose Maria Olazabal, with an eagle at the 15th, won the 1994 Masters by two strokes over Tom Lehman for the first of his two green jackets.

17.title: Tiger Woods's chip-in for the ages
17.text: Tiger Woods's iconic chip-in at the 16th, the ball pausing on the lip before dropping, helped seal his fourth Masters title in 2005 this week.

17.title: Charl Schwartzel wins the Masters
17.text: South African Charl Schwartzel birdied the final four holes to win the 2011 Masters by two strokes over Adam Scott and Jason Day.

17.title: Jordan Spieth wins RBC Heritage in playoff
17.text: Jordan Spieth got up and down from a bunker to beat Patrick Cantlay on the first playoff hole and win the 2022 RBC Heritage at Harbour Town.

18.title: Claude Harmon wins the Masters
18.text: Club professional Claude Harmon won the 1948 Masters by five strokes over Cary Middlecoff, the last club pro to win a major championship.

18.title: Nicklaus's record stands
18.text: Jack Nicklaus's record 271 at the 1965 Masters prompted Bobby Jones to say Nicklaus played 'a game with which I am not familiar,' a mark that stood for decades.

18.title: Olazabal's emotional comeback win
18.text: Jose Maria Olazabal completed his return from a foot ailment that had threatened his career to win the 1999 Masters this week at Augusta National.

18.title: Mickelson's leap of joy
18.text: Phil Mickelson's leap after his winning birdie putt at the 2004 Masters ended his long search for a first major and became an enduring image this week.

18.title: Mickelson's shot from the pine straw
18.text: Phil Mickelson's daring 6-iron from the pine straw between two trees to the 13th green helped seal his third Masters title this week in 2010.

18.title: Stewart Cink wins RBC Heritage
18.text: Stewart Cink, 47, completed a wire-to-wire victory to win the 2021 RBC Heritage at Harbour Town for his third title there and second win of the season.

19.title: Sam Snead wins second Masters
19.text: Sam Snead won the 1952 Masters by four strokes over Jack Burke Jr. for the second of his three green jackets.

19.title: Art Wall's birdie barrage
19.text: Art Wall Jr.'s five birdies over the final six holes to win the 1959 Masters this week was one of the era's most stunning closing rushes.

19.title: Watson's second green jacket
19.text: Tom Watson completed his second Masters victory in 1981 this week, holding off a charging Jack Nicklaus down the stretch.

19.title: Mize's miracle chip
19.text: Larry Mize's 140-foot chip-in at the 11th to beat Greg Norman in the 1987 Masters playoff remains one of the most shocking shots in major history.

19.title: Couples's charmed Masters
19.text: Fred Couples won the 1992 Masters this week after his tee shot at the par-3 12th miraculously stayed on the bank above Rae's Creek.

19.title: Cabrera's playoff Masters
19.text: Angel Cabrera survived a three-man sudden-death playoff to win the 2009 Masters this week, the first South American Masters champion.

20.title: Jimmy Demaret's second Masters
20.text: Jimmy Demaret completed his second Masters victory in 1947 this week, edging Byron Nelson and amateur Frank Stranahan by two strokes.

20.title: Palmer's first green jacket
20.text: Arnold Palmer won his first Masters in 1958 this week, a victory that helped launch the 'Arnie's Army' phenomenon and golf's television boom.

20.title: Seve's youthful triumph
20.text: Seve Ballesteros completed his four-stroke 1980 Masters win this week at age 23, becoming the first European and youngest champion at the time.

20.title: Nicklaus's roar at Augusta
20.text: The roars that followed Jack Nicklaus's 1986 back-nine charge to a sixth Masters at age 46 remain among the most famous sounds in golf.

20.title: Tiger's era begins
20.text: Tiger Woods's record-shattering 1997 Masters victory transformed golf's popularity, demographics and prize money over the following decades.

20.title: Matt Kuchar wins RBC Heritage
20.text: Matt Kuchar shot a final-round 64 to win the 2014 RBC Heritage at Harbour Town by two strokes for one of his nine PGA Tour titles.

21.title: Horton Smith wins first Masters
21.text: Horton Smith won the inaugural Masters Tournament, then called the Augusta National Invitation, by one stroke over Craig Wood in 1934.

21.title: Doug Ford's bunker holeout
21.text: Doug Ford's holeout from a greenside bunker on the 72nd hole to win the 1957 Masters this week remains one of the great closing shots at Augusta.

21.title: Langer breaks through
21.text: Bernhard Langer completed his first Masters win in 1985 this week, the start of a German golf surge and a Hall of Fame career across multiple tours.

21.title: Ian Woosnam wins the Masters
21.text: Welshman Ian Woosnam made a clutch par at the 18th to win the 1991 Masters by one stroke over Jose Maria Olazabal, reaching World No. 1.

21.title: Faldo overtakes Norman
21.text: Nick Faldo's 1996 Masters win, completed this week as Greg Norman squandered a six-shot lead, stands as one of the most dramatic final days in golf.

21.title: Adam Scott wins the Masters
21.text: Adam Scott beat Angel Cabrera on the second playoff hole in the rain to win the 2013 Masters, becoming the first Australian to claim a green jacket.

22.title: Sarazen's Grand Slam complete
22.text: Gene Sarazen's 1935 Masters playoff victory completed his career Grand Slam, making him the first golfer to win all four professional majors.

22.title: Burke's record comeback
22.text: Jack Burke Jr.'s 1956 Masters win from eight back on the final day remains the largest final-round comeback in tournament history.

22.title: Aaron's lone major
22.text: Tommy Aaron completed his only major victory at the 1973 Masters this week, five years after his scorekeeping error helped decide the 1968 title.

22.title: Faldo's back-to-back
22.text: Nick Faldo completed back-to-back Masters titles in 1990 this week, beating Ray Floyd in a playoff at the 11th hole in the fading light.

22.title: The Tiger Slam
22.text: Tiger Woods's 2001 Masters victory completed his hold on all four professional major titles simultaneously, a feat unmatched in the modern era.

22.title: Bubba's first major
22.text: Bubba Watson's 2012 Masters playoff win, sealed by his hooking wedge from the trees, gave the self-taught left-hander his first major title.

23.title: Demaret's record third Masters
23.text: Jimmy Demaret's 1950 Masters win made him the first three-time champion at Augusta National, a record later surpassed by Arnold Palmer and Jack Nicklaus.

23.title: Brewer's redemption
23.text: Gay Brewer won the 1967 Masters this week, a year after losing a playoff for the title, for his only major championship.

23.title: Faldo's first Masters
23.text: Nick Faldo won his first Masters in 1989 this week, beating Scott Hoch in a playoff at the 11th hole in near-darkness.

23.title: Ben Crenshaw wins emotional Masters
23.text: Ben Crenshaw, days after the death of his mentor Harvey Penick, won the 1995 Masters by one stroke over Davis Love III in a tearful triumph.

23.title: Singh's green jacket
23.text: Vijay Singh's 2000 Masters victory this week added a green jacket to his major collection during his rise to World No. 1.

23.title: Mickelson's second Masters
23.text: Phil Mickelson won the 2006 Masters this week for his second green jacket, the peak of a stretch in which he won three majors in three years.

24.title: Middlecoff's record win
24.text: Cary Middlecoff's seven-stroke 1955 Masters victory, aided by a famous 80-foot eagle putt, set a winning-margin record that stood until Tiger Woods in 1997.

24.title: Nicklaus defends at Augusta
24.text: Jack Nicklaus became the first player to successfully defend a Masters title in 1966 this week, winning an 18-hole playoff over Tommy Jacobs and Gay Brewer.

24.title: Watson outduels Nicklaus
24.text: Tom Watson's 1977 Masters victory over Jack Nicklaus this week was a prelude to their famous 'Duel in the Sun' at Turnberry that summer.

24.title: Seve's second Masters
24.text: Seve Ballesteros's blistering start to the final round of the 1983 Masters, with birdies early, carried him to a four-stroke win this week.

24.title: Olazabal's first green jacket
24.text: Jose Maria Olazabal's eagle at the 15th propelled him to his first Masters title in 1994 this week, the second Spaniard after Seve to win at Augusta.

24.title: Schwartzel's closing birdies
24.text: Charl Schwartzel birdied the last four holes to win the 2011 Masters this week, a finish that capped a dramatic Sunday at Augusta National.

25.title: Byron Nelson wins first Masters
25.text: Byron Nelson made up six strokes in two holes on Ralph Guldahl to win the 1937 Masters, the first of his two green jackets.

25.title: Harmon's club-pro triumph
25.text: Claude Harmon's five-stroke 1948 Masters win made him the last club professional to capture a major championship.

25.title: Snead beats Hogan in playoff
25.text: Sam Snead's one-stroke playoff win over Ben Hogan at the 1954 Masters this week was a clash of two of golf's greatest rivals.

25.title: Coody's lone major
25.text: Charles Coody's late birdies to win the 1971 Masters this week gave him his only major championship after a near-miss the year before.

25.title: Stadler's playoff win
25.text: Craig Stadler held off Dan Pohl in sudden death to win the 1982 Masters this week, the 'Walrus' claiming his only major.

25.title: Mickelson's first major leap
25.text: Phil Mickelson's leap of joy after his winning putt at the 2004 Masters ended his long search for a first major and became an enduring image.

26.title: Hogan's record Masters
26.text: Ben Hogan's record 274 at the 1953 Masters launched a season in which he won the Masters, U.S. Open and Open Championship, the 'Hogan Slam.'

26.title: Wall's birdie rush
26.text: Art Wall Jr.'s five birdies over the final six holes to win the 1959 Masters was one of the era's most stunning closing rushes.

26.title: Casper's playoff Masters
26.text: Billy Casper beat Gene Littler in an 18-hole playoff to win the 1970 Masters this week for his lone green jacket.

26.title: Mize stuns Norman
26.text: Larry Mize's improbable chip-in to win the 1987 Masters this week denied Greg Norman in heartbreaking fashion at the second playoff hole.

26.title: Couples's charmed week
26.text: Fred Couples won the 1992 Masters this week, his ball's refusal to roll into Rae's Creek at the 12th becoming a lasting symbol of his charmed run.

26.title: O'Meara's late birdies
26.text: Mark O'Meara birdied the 17th and 18th to win the 1998 Masters this week, capturing his first major at age 41 in a breakout season.

27.title: Henry Picard wins the Masters
27.text: Henry Picard rallied over the closing holes to win the 1938 Masters by two strokes over Harry Cooper and Ralph Guldahl.

27.title: Snead's green jacket era begins
27.text: Sam Snead's 1949 Masters win was the first to feature the awarding of the green jacket to the champion, a tradition that continues today.

27.title: Palmer's disputed ruling
27.text: Arnold Palmer's first Masters in 1958 hinged on a disputed embedded-ball ruling at the 12th, which the rules ultimately resolved in his favor.

27.title: Archer's putting masterclass
27.text: George Archer's superb putting carried him to the 1969 Masters title this week by a single stroke over a crowded leaderboard.

27.title: The great 1975 duel
27.text: Jack Nicklaus, Tom Weiskopf and Johnny Miller staged one of Augusta's greatest Sunday duels in 1975, with Nicklaus prevailing by one for his fifth Masters.

27.title: Nicklaus's sixth green jacket
27.text: Jack Nicklaus's 1986 Masters win at age 46, sealed by a back-nine 30, remains the most celebrated victory of his career and Augusta's history.

28.title: Jimmy Demaret wins first Masters
28.text: Jimmy Demaret won his first Masters in 1940, shooting a then-record 30 on the back nine en route to a four-stroke victory.

28.title: Herman Keiser wins the Masters
28.text: Herman Keiser, the 'Missouri Mortician,' held off a charging Ben Hogan to win the 1946 Masters by one stroke, the first edition after World War II.

28.title: Hal Sutton born
28.text: American Hal Sutton, the 1983 PGA Championship and 1983 and 2000 Players champion who famously beat Tiger Woods at TPC Sawgrass, was born on this day.

28.title: Nicklaus's youngest-champion mark
28.text: Jack Nicklaus's 1963 Masters win at age 23 made him the youngest champion at the time, a mark later broken by Seve Ballesteros and Tiger Woods.

28.title: The scorecard that decided a Masters
28.text: Roberto De Vicenzo's signed scorecard error at the 1968 Masters, which handed the title to Bob Goalby, prompted his famous lament, 'What a stupid I am.'

28.title: Norman's Sunday collapse
28.text: Greg Norman's six-stroke lead evaporating in a closing 78 at the 1996 Masters, as Nick Faldo shot 67, remains one of golf's most painful final-day stories.

29.title: Augusta's first champion
29.text: Horton Smith's victory in the inaugural 1934 Masters launched what would become one of golf's four major championships and its most tradition-rich event.

29.title: Hogan's first green jacket
29.text: Ben Hogan's 1951 Masters win was the first of his two green jackets, part of a remarkable comeback after his near-fatal 1949 car crash.

29.title: Palmer's playoff charge
29.text: Arnold Palmer's playoff win over Gary Player and Dow Finsterwald at the 1962 Masters was another chapter in the 'Palmer charge' lore at Augusta.

29.title: Fuzzy Zoeller wins the Masters
29.text: Fuzzy Zoeller beat Ed Sneed and Tom Watson in the first sudden-death playoff in Masters history to win the 1979 title as a rookie at Augusta.

29.title: Lyle's bunker shot for the ages
29.text: Sandy Lyle's 7-iron from the fairway bunker at the 18th, then the birdie putt to win the 1988 Masters, ranks among the greatest pressure shots in major history.

29.title: Zach Johnson's lay-up strategy
29.text: Zach Johnson's decision to lay up on every par-5 in the cold, windy 2007 Masters carried him to a one-over-par winning total and his first major.

30.title: Nelson's Masters charge
30.text: Byron Nelson's birdie-eagle on the 12th and 13th to overtake Ralph Guldahl at the 1937 Masters gave him the first of his two green jackets.

30.title: Byron Nelson wins second Masters
30.text: Byron Nelson beat Ben Hogan in an 18-hole playoff to win the 1942 Masters, the last edition before the tournament paused for World War II.

30.title: Palmer's final green jacket
30.text: Arnold Palmer's six-stroke win at the 1964 Masters was his fourth and last green jacket, cementing his standing as Augusta's defining figure of the era.

30.title: Crenshaw's tearful triumph
30.text: Ben Crenshaw's 1995 Masters win, days after burying his mentor Harvey Penick, ended with the champion bent over in tears on the 18th green.

30.title: Adam Scott ends Australia's wait
30.text: Adam Scott's playoff victory at the 2013 Masters made him the first Australian to win at Augusta, ending decades of close calls for his country.

30.title: Garcia's long-awaited major
30.text: Sergio Garcia's 2017 Masters win, on what would have been Seve Ballesteros's 60th birthday, ended nearly two decades of major heartbreak for the Spaniard.
